title: cargo leptos end-to-end fails 5 tests due to stale test fixtures
description:
cargo leptos end-to-end consistently fails 5 tests that pass in scripts/verify (via nix build .#nix-only-checks):

- tests/auth.spec.ts:49 login with valid credentials
- tests/auth.spec.ts:85 logout page logs out
- tests/auth.spec.ts:101 sidebar reverts to signed-out state
- tests/email.spec.ts:6 email verification flow
- tests/password_reset.spec.ts:6 password reset flow

Root cause: the local end2end/run-e2e.sh does:

    "$BIN" user-create --username testlogin --password testpassword123 2>/dev/null || true

The 2>/dev/null swallows any error. The Nix VM's seed_db() instead:
1. Deletes from users/sessions/invites/email_verifications/password_resets/posts/tags/site_config.
2. Sets registration_policy = open.
3. Creates testlogin AND testnoemail with passwords.
4. Removes mail.jsonl.

The local dev DB (data/jaunder.db) accumulates state across runs (708 stale users observed). testlogin can end up missing or with a non-matching password after, e.g., the password_reset test runs. user-create silently fails (e.g., 'user already exists' or because the running server has locked the row), the script proceeds, and every test that logs in as testlogin fails with 'invalid credentials'.
design: Make end2end/run-e2e.sh mirror Nix seed_db(): wipe stateful tables, set registration_policy, create both fixture users, and remove mail.jsonl. Stop swallowing user-create stderr so a real failure is visible. Cargo-leptos's auto-init creates the schema but doesn't wipe data, so the seeding must do that explicitly.
acceptance_criteria:
- cargo leptos end-to-end finishes with 0 failed.
- Output matches the Nix-VM e2e run (same set of tests pass).
- run-e2e.sh prints user-create errors on stderr rather than hiding them.
close_reason: Fixed via scripts/e2e-local.sh wrapper (per-run temp storage dir) + shared scripts/seed-e2e-fixtures.sh (used by local run-e2e.sh and both Nix e2e checks). cargo leptos end-to-end via the wrapper now passes 41/41 in 9.8s. Also revisited jaunder-3gt: the LocalResource fix was rolled back in favor of defensive use_context in backup.rs (LocalResource awaited in SSR-rendered Suspense hangs the Suspense — see updated memory).

title: Fix tokio-rt-worker panics in backup.rs: SSR Resource fetchers race with owner disposal
description:
Two tokio-rt-worker panics observed in cargo leptos end-to-end:

- web/src/backup.rs:138 (backup_warning_visible) — called via Resource in BackupBanner
- web/src/backup.rs:195 (current_user_is_operator) — called via Resource in Sidebar

Both: 'expected context of type Arc<AppState> to be present'. Same root-cause family as jaunder-nba: the Resource fetcher runs on a tokio worker, and when the per-request reactive owner is disposed (client disconnect, e2e parallelism) the AppState context provided per-owner is gone, so expect_context panics.

These two server fns are uniquely vulnerable because BackupBanner and Sidebar render on every page — eager Resource fetches multiply the race odds. Other server fns using the same expect_context pattern are only invoked via /api/* HTTP, which has its own per-request context provision and no owner-disposal window.
design:
Structural fix (user-chosen): make these Resources client-only by switching from Resource::new to LocalResource::new. Server-side renders without the operator-gated UI; client fills it in after hydration.

User impact:
- Unauthenticated visitors: no change (never saw operator UI).
- Authenticated non-operators: no change.
- Authenticated operators: brief moment after first paint where BackupBanner and operator-conditional sidebar nav items are absent, then pop in after hydration.

Sites to convert:
- web/src/pages/ui.rs:130 (BackupBanner: visible Resource)
- web/src/pages/ui.rs:688-691 (Sidebar: operator Resource)

The companion 'user' Resource in Sidebar uses current_user(), which goes through require_auth() / use_context::<Parts>() (graceful Option path, no panic) — leave it alone.
acceptance_criteria:
- cargo leptos end-to-end shows zero 'tokio-rt-worker.*panicked' lines.
- SSR HTML for unauthenticated and authenticated-non-operator visitors is byte-identical (or trivially equivalent) to pre-fix.
- Authenticated operators still see BackupBanner (when config incomplete) and operator nav items after hydration.
- scripts/verify passes.
close_reason: Fixed in d1353b7. cargo leptos end-to-end: tokio-rt-worker panics 2 -> 0. 36 passed, 5 failed (all pre-existing flakes — auth login/logout, email/password_reset email-capture timing — out of scope per user).

title: Fix tokio-rt-worker panics: Effect::new_isomorphic races with SSR owner disposal
description:
During cargo leptos end-to-end, 22 panics fire from reactive_graph-0.2.13/src/traits.rs:361 with messages like "At web/src/pages/home.rs:65:30, you tried to access a reactive value which was defined at web/src/pages/home.rs:29:17, but it has already been disposed." All on tokio-rt-worker threads (server-side SSR). Root cause: four sites use Effect::new_isomorphic to copy a Resource's resolved value into RwSignals. When the Resource future resolves after the per-request reactive owner is disposed, the effect re-fires server-side and panics on get_untracked/set against the dead signal.

Sites:
- web/src/pages/home.rs:49
- web/src/pages/posts.rs:228
- web/src/pages/posts.rs:249
- web/src/pages/posts.rs:423

Note: only home.rs currently panics in practice; posts.rs sites share the bug latently.
design:
Approach (Option A): stop copying Resource->RwSignal inside an isomorphic effect. Instead:
1. Render initial state directly from the Resource inside <Suspense> so SSR still has data.
2. Keep RwSignals only for state genuinely mutated client-side (appended rows from 'load more', error flashes, cursor state for pagination).
3. Use a client-only Effect::new (no isomorphic) to seed/reset those signals when the Resource refreshes.

This removes the server-side disposal race entirely because:
- Suspense reads the Resource value within the live reactive owner during rendering.
- The mutation signals only need to exist on the client, where there is no async-after-disposal scenario.
acceptance_criteria:
- cargo leptos end-to-end output contains zero 'tokio-rt-worker.*panicked' lines (other test failures out of scope).
- scripts/verify passes.
- SSR still renders timeline rows for both authenticated home feed and unauthenticated local timeline (curl '/' shows post HTML without JS execution).
- 'Load more', refresh-after-publish, and error paths still behave correctly.
notes:
Observed during e2e run on branch 'feeds' on 2026-05-11. Evidence in /tmp/leptos-e2e.log (panic lines: 136, 141, 146, 149, 155, 159, 173, 177, 183, 191, 194, 218, 222, 228, 261, 266, 272, 275, 278, 282, 285, 370). User chose Option A + scope all four sites.

Post-fix follow-up: record two bd memories — (a) anti-pattern: Effect::new_isomorphic copying Resource into RwSignals races with SSR owner disposal; (b) preferred pattern: <Suspense>+resource.get() for initial render, RwSignals for client-side mutation only, client-only Effect::new for seeding. Reference post-fix home.rs and posts.rs as canonical examples.
close_reason: Fixed in 6ba0c03 (home.rs) and 199ec9c (posts.rs). cargo leptos end-to-end: tokio-rt-worker disposal panics 22 -> 0. No regressions traceable to refactor (remaining test failures are pre-existing flakes: DB locks, email-capture timing, auth flow).

title: M8.7: Feed-event worker (regen + WebSub ping)
description:
See docs/milestones/M8.md Step 7.

title: web: add a theme selector to the UI
description:
The codebase ships three CSS themes (terminal, studio, reader — see server/assets/jaunder-themes.css) and the .j-root element is wired to a theme RwSignal with persistence to localStorage (web/src/pages/mod.rs ~line 85-110). The user has no way to choose a theme: DEFAULT_THEME is the only path that ever sets a value, and there is no settings UI, sidebar control, or topbar menu that exposes it.

Proposal: add a small theme picker — probably a j-seg segmented control in /profile or a topbar dropdown in AppShell — that lets the user choose terminal / studio / reader. The wiring already persists the chosen theme via the existing localStorage hook; the picker just needs to call theme.set(...).

Related: jaunder-ka4 — on the deployed binary the data-theme attribute apparently isn't reaching the DOM, so right now even the DEFAULT_THEME is silently absent. Fixing jaunder-ka4 makes the picker meaningful; shipping the picker without jaunder-ka4 would let users select a value that the running server fails to apply.

title: bug(ui): .j-root data-theme attribute is missing on deployed binary
description:
On the nixos-deployed binary, the .j-root element appears to render WITHOUT a data-theme attribute (or with an empty one). Symptoms:
- DevTools shows no --err custom property on the .j-root computed-styles list
- --on-accent still resolves (because :root in jaunder.css supplies it as a fallback) while theme-only tokens silently disappear
- Surfaced visually as jaunder-bxo (filled-red Delete buttons rendering invisibly until --err was backfilled into :root)

Expected: <div class="j-root" data-theme="studio"> on first paint so .j-root[data-theme="studio"] in jaunder-themes.css applies and the full theme variable pack cascades.

Current wiring (web/src/pages/mod.rs):
  let theme = RwSignal::new(DEFAULT_THEME.to_string());
  view! { <div class="j-root" attr:data-theme=move || theme.get()> ... }

Hypotheses to check:
1. attr:<name> reactive bindings may not render the attribute during SSR if the closure produces an empty string at server time (e.g., signal default is dropped between SSR and hydration).
2. The localStorage restore path may set theme to an empty string under some condition before provide_context runs.
3. The deployed binary build path may strip the attr binding (production-only compile flag?).

Fix direction: render data-theme as a static attribute when the value is non-empty (split SSR from client-side reactive update), and/or assert during render that DEFAULT_THEME is non-empty. As a defense-in-depth measure, the :root fallback pack should mirror every token any theme defines, so missing data-theme degrades gracefully instead of breaking specific rules silently.

title: web: unify row-action button styling across list/card pages
description:
Row-action buttons disagree across pages:
- DraftsPage row: Edit/Publish/Delete all 'j-btn is-ghost'
- PostCard (PostPage): Edit/Unpublish/Delete all plain 'j-btn'
- DraftPreviewPage: Publish 'j-btn is-primary', Delete 'j-btn is-ghost'
- MediaPage row (single Delete): 'j-btn is-ghost'
- SessionsPage row (single Revoke): 'j-btn is-ghost'

Style guide §5 says 'is-ghost — Destructive or table-row actions (Delete, Publish)'. DraftsPage / MediaPage / SessionsPage follow the guide; PostCard and DraftPreviewPage drift from it.

Pick a canonical pattern and propagate, then update docs/web-style-guide.md §5 accordingly. Two viable options:
1. Keep current guide (all row actions ghost). Fix PostCard and DraftPreviewPage to match Drafts/Media/Sessions.
2. Differentiate: neutral row actions (Edit, Publish) get plain j-btn; only destructive (Delete, Unpublish) get is-ghost (or introduce is-danger). Fix Drafts/Media/Sessions to match this richer convention. Visually communicates intent.

Note: this issue is closely related to jaunder-h1u (consolidate DraftPreviewPage + PostPage) and jaunder-urw (Unpublish-on-draft); resolving the row-button style first lets those two land cleanly.
notes: Concrete instance on the Media page (/media): the MediaUploadButton renders 'class="j-btn"' (plain) while per-row Delete buttons render 'class="j-btn is-ghost"'. The two button styles sitting next to each other on a single page makes the inconsistency obvious. Any chosen unification needs to resolve this within-page divergence too.
close_reason: Closed

title: bug(ui): PostCard shows 'Unpublish' on draft permalinks; should be 'Publish'
description:
PostCard in web/src/pages/ui.rs renders an Unpublish button whenever is_author is true. PostPage uses PostCard for both published and draft posts, so visiting a draft's permalink as the author surfaces 'Unpublish' on something that is already unpublished — clicking it triggers UnpublishPost which is a no-op or error.

Fix: PostCard should adapt its action column based on draft state. When the post is a draft (post.is_draft or equivalent passed in), render 'Publish' (dispatching PublishPost) instead of 'Unpublish'. Edit and Delete remain unchanged.

Note: post.is_draft isn't currently on TimelinePostSummary; either thread it through (preferred) or derive from published_at being empty. PostPage already computes the draft banner from fetched.is_draft, so the data is available — pass it explicitly to PostCard.

This bug largely disappears if jaunder-h1u (consolidate the two pages) is done by giving PostCard the draft-aware action column it should have had, so wire the action-column fix as part of that consolidation rather than patching twice.

title: web: consolidate DraftPreviewPage and PostPage into a single view
description:
Two pages render essentially the same post:

- /draft/:post_id/preview (DraftPreviewPage) — uses PostDisplay + a custom action column with [Publish, Delete]. Drafts only.
- /~user/YYYY/MM/DD/slug (PostPage) — uses PostCard with [Edit, Unpublish, Delete]. Handles both published and draft posts.

The Drafts listing in DraftsPage links to BOTH a 'Preview' (DraftPreviewPage) and a 'Permalink' (PostPage) for the same draft, surfacing arbitrary differences in chrome and actions. Both already share PostDisplay underneath; the only real divergence is the action column.

Proposal: drop DraftPreviewPage and route /draft/:post_id/preview to PostPage (after resolving post_id → permalink server-side, or by letting PostCard accept a post_id-keyed load path). Update the Drafts listing to link to the canonical permalink only. Folds the fix for the 'Unpublish on a draft' bug (see follow-up issue) into PostCard's action column so it adapts to draft state.

title: web: compose toolbar layout — group inline composer's action buttons
description:
The compact PostCreateForm rendered by InlineComposer (authenticated home page) lays out its children as a single flex row inside `.j-composer-body`:

  [textarea] [j-seg Markdown|Org] [j-composer-toolbar Save-draft + Publish stacked] [MediaPanel attach]

The format toggle is horizontal, the action buttons are stacked, and the media-attach button sits in a third column — three different control geometries side-by-side. The result reads as scattered controls.

Redesign so the textarea spans the full body width (no flex siblings to its right) and a single horizontal toolbar row underneath the textarea contains, left-to-right: format toggle, attach-media button, spacer, Save draft, Publish (primary). Update .j-composer-body / .j-composer-toolbar accordingly in jaunder.css and rework the compact branch of PostCreateForm in web/src/pages/ui.rs.

value: When scripts/e2e-local.sh or cargo leptos end-to-end exhibits unexplained test failures (especially auth/register failing in ways that don't track to a code change), check for lingering jaunder processes from previous runs (`pgrep -f 'target.*jaunder'`). The local wrapper creates a per-run temp DB, but an old jaunder process bound to port 3000 keeps serving requests against the OLD database, causing register/login/post-creation tests to fail mysteriously while the build itself looks clean. Always `pkill -f 'target.*jaunder' || true` before retrying e2e locally.

Local cargo leptos end-to-end uses scripts/e2e-local.sh wrapper (per-run temp storage dir) so the test suite starts from clean state, matching the Nix VM e2e environment under scripts/verify. The wrapper exports JAUNDER_STORAGE_PATH, JAUNDER_DB, JAUNDER_DB_PATH, JAUNDER_MAIL_CAPTURE_FILE to a mktemp dir and cleans up on exit. end2end/run-e2e.sh and the Nix flake's seed_db both delegate fixture creation (testlogin, testnoemail, registration_policy=open, clear mail capture) to scripts/seed-e2e-fixtures.sh — single source of truth. Don't reintroduce hardcoded DELETE FROM table SQL in the seed step; the temp-dir-per-run approach makes that unnecessary.

Anti-pattern: Effect::new_isomorphic copying Resource value into RwSignals causes SSR tokio-rt-worker panics. Why: the Resource future can resolve on a tokio worker after the per-request reactive owner is disposed, and the isomorphic effect fires post-disposal, calling get_untracked/set on a dead signal and panicking with 'you tried to access a reactive value which was defined at ..., but it has already been disposed' (reactive_graph traits.rs:361). How to spot: any Effect::new_isomorphic(|_| { if let Some(x) = resource.try_get().flatten() { signal.set(...) } }). Originally observed at web/src/pages/home.rs:49 and web/src/pages/posts.rs:228,249,423 — fixed in commits 6ba0c03 and 199ec9c on the 'feeds' branch (2026-05-11).

Anti-pattern (SSR-eager Resource calling expect_context::<Arc<AppState>>): when a Leptos Resource invoking a server fn is rendered eagerly on every page (e.g. BackupBanner, Sidebar), the Resource fetcher runs on a tokio worker via Suspense, and on client disconnect / e2e parallelism the per-request reactive owner can be disposed before the worker reaches the expect_context call — panic 'expected context of type Arc<AppState> to be present'. Fix: replace expect_context::<Arc<AppState>>() with a small helper that returns InternalResult (use_context::<Arc<AppState>>().ok_or_else(|| InternalError::server_message("missing AppState context"))). The error path is already harmless (Sidebar/BackupBanner treat Err as 'not operator / no banner'). Do NOT switch to LocalResource as a structural fix — LocalResource awaited inside an SSR-rendered Suspense never resolves on the server, hanging the entire Suspense subtree and breaking many e2e tests with hydration timeouts. Reference: web/src/backup.rs after jaunder-3gt revisit; jaunder-3gt closed by defensive use_context, not by LocalResource.

Preferred pattern for Resource-derived initial state with client-side mutation: use plain Effect::new (NOT Effect::new_isomorphic) to copy a Resource's resolved value into RwSignals, and ALWAYS wrap the Effect::new in #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]. Even a plain Effect::new runs its closure once initially on the server during SSR, and can rerun if the resource resolves before SSR finishes. This causes random/flaky server-side test coverage (e.g., in home.rs or posts.rs). Wrapping with #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")] completely strips it from server-side compilation, ensuring 100% deterministic server-side test coverage and avoiding any server-side execution. Keep original-style conditional-set patterns (e.g. 'if mode != new_mode { mode.set(new_mode) }') to prevent spurious signal updates from re-running outer view closures and remounting child components.
